office_does: County commissioners manage county finances and property, adopt ordinances and budgets, and oversee county services.
race_blurb: Incumbent Robert Dean faces Kevin Berry and Jeremiah Adams, who emphasize rural economic opportunity, infrastructure, and local investment.

candidates.summary: Adams has nonprofit, industrial, maintenance, and community-organization experience and proposes using existing county resources for cottage industries and youth retention.
highlights: Proposes county investment focused on residents, cottage industries, and youth retention [S1].
Has nonprofit-management, industrial-supervision, and community-organization experience [S1].

spending.basis: He explicitly proposes using existing county resources for resident-focused economic investment.

experience.basis: He brings varied management and community experience but no documented elected county record.

candidates.summary: Berry is an Army veteran and former business manager and truck driver emphasizing jobs, small businesses, roads, infrastructure, and transparent budgeting.
highlights: Prioritizes local jobs, small businesses, rural industries, roads, and infrastructure [S1].
Calls for responsible and transparent budgeting [S1].

experience.basis: His military and private-sector management record is substantial but outside elected county government.

candidates.summary: Dean is the incumbent commissioner with intergovernmental board experience who emphasizes fiscal responsibility, efficiency, transparency, and continuity.
highlights: Has served three and a half years as commissioner and on several regional boards [S1][S2].
Emphasizes fiscal responsibility, government efficiency, transparent fund management, and taxpayer respect [S1].

spending.basis: His stated governing approach centers on fiscal responsibility, efficiency, taxpayer restraint, and transparent fund management.

experience.basis: Three and a half years as commissioner plus regional-board service establish a proven institutional record.
